summary: Dan Shapiro shares a reader comment following up on his earlier review of the SawStop table saw, a safety-focused power tool. The reader, Dave Wright, reports owning the saw and points to early reviews from woodworkers who purchased it themselves. Wright notes that while expensive, the SawStop's premium is comparable to upgrading trim levels on an SUV — a matter of priorities rather than unreasonable cost. The post serves as a brief community update on an emerging product.
key_points: Early real-world reviews of the SawStop table saw were beginning to surface from woodworkers who bought it with their own money
The SawStop's higher price is framed as comparable to paying for a premium vehicle trim — a reasonable upgrade for those who prioritize safety
The post highlights community engagement and reader expertise as valuable follow-up to product commentary
money_quotes: It's expensive, but you'll pay about the same to get the Eddie Bauer version of an SUV instead of the regular one. For most people it's just a matter of priorities.
key_insight: The SawStop table saw's safety premium is comparable to a vehicle trim upgrade, making it a matter of personal priorities rather than prohibitive cost.
